questionText: A 15 HP, 460V, 3-phase motor has an FLA of 21A. The motor has a 1.15 service factor. What is the maximum overload protection setting?
explanation: Per NEC 430.32(A)(1), motors with service factor ≥ 1.15 can have overload protection up to 125% of nameplate FLA. Using the formula FLA: 21A × 1.25 = 26.25A.
sparkyTip: Service factor 1.15+ or 40°C rise = 125% overload. Otherwise it's 115%. This protects the motor windings!
